name: Glen Canyon Park

permits.description: site plan for phace ii. renovation, seismic retrofit of (e) building. addition of a multi-purpose room & bleacher. new lobby- construction, mep, fire alarm and sprinklesr separate permits.

to make (ada) acessible: path of travel. restrooms, doors, drinking foundation, mechanical, electrical work to the site & restrooms, drinking fountain. incl conc. slab in restrooms. new landscape work to the site.

fire only permit. install new 8" fire service & new low pressure fire hydrant off of 8" city main on berkeley way per nfpa 13 & 24. new low pressure fire hydrant in glenn canyon park. fire hydrant location approx on north west corner of gynmasium
install fire sprinkler system overhead to nfpa13. - 195 heads. ref #2014-0624-9343. underground on deferred submittal.
underground fire line, ref. sprinkler permit # 2016 0309 1589 ref #2014-0624-9343.

summary_en: Glen Canyon Park's civic record centers on renovation. The 2008 Clean & Safe Neighborhood Parks Bond describes a 66.6-acre park off Elk Street and O'Shaughnessy Blvd, with a 1934 recreation center and the 1960 Silver Tree Day Camp, and funds repair of the rec center, play area, day camp, courts and field. Arts Commission minutes record selecting three finalists for the Glen Canyon Park Recreation Center public-art project. Mid-century redevelopment reports describe an 80-acre naturally wooded canyon with a day camp. Building permits for the park total about $9.8 million, led by an $8.2 million 2015 phase-II renovation and seismic retrofit. The park is operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line totals about $250M.
